note: All recipe (response-graded) — no AI provider/API key needed. Each probes an ai_ckeditor architecture fact an unfamiliar agent would otherwise have to read src/ and the *.yml to answer.

evals.prompt: In the Drupal ai_ckeditor module, I want to add my own AI action to the editor (e.g. 'Expand'). What plugin type do I implement, in which Plugin/ namespace, and which attribute annotates it?
evals.expected_output: Implement an AiCKEditor plugin under Plugin/AiCKEditor with the #[AiCKEditor] attribute; extend AiCKEditorPluginBase. Managed by plugin.manager.ai_ckeditor.

evals.prompt: How do I turn on the AI tools in a specific text format in ai_ckeditor? Is there a dedicated /admin/config/ai settings page for it?
evals.expected_output: No dedicated route (configure is null). Edit a CKEditor 5 text format at /admin/config/content/formats, drag the 'AI CKEditor' button into the active toolbar, then enable individual action plugins in the 'AI tools' section.

evals.prompt: In ai_ckeditor, which permission machine name must a role have to use the AI editor tools? Give the exact string.
evals.expected_output: use ai ckeditor — it is the _permission on the ai_ckeditor.dialog and ai_ckeditor.do_request routes.

evals.prompt: When an editor triggers an ai_ckeditor action, how is the AI provider/model chosen? What happens if the action has no provider configured?
evals.expected_output: The controller uses the per-action provider stored in the editor config; if none is set it falls back to AI Core's default 'chat' provider via getDefaultProviderForOperationType('chat').

evals.prompt: In ai_ckeditor, which route/controller actually performs the AI request and streams text back to the editor, and what operation type does it call?
evals.expected_output: Route ai_ckeditor.do_request (/api/ai-ckeditor/request/{editor}/{ai_ckeditor_plugin}) -> Controller\AiRequest::doRequest builds a ChatInput/ChatMessage and calls the chat operation, streaming the response.

evals.prompt: Two of the built-in ai_ckeditor action plugins only appear when another module is installed. Which plugins, and what makes them conditionally available?
evals.expected_output: Tone (ai_ckeditor_tone) and Translate (ai_ckeditor_translate) declare module_dependencies: ['taxonomy']; the plugin manager's findDefinitions() drops plugins whose module_dependencies aren't installed.

evals.prompt: Where are the default prompt templates for the ai_ckeditor actions (summarize, translate, tone, etc.) stored, and how would I edit the summarize prompt?
evals.expected_output: In the ai_ckeditor.settings config object under the 'prompts' mapping (keys: complete, modify_prompt, reformat, spellfix, summarise, tone, translate). Edit e.g. drush config:set ai_ckeditor.settings prompts.summarise '...'.

evals.prompt: Besides the toolbar button, ai_ckeditor offers another way to reach AI actions from a text selection. What is it and how is it enabled?
evals.expected_output: A contextual balloon menu (ai_ckeditor_balloon_menu / 'AI Balloon Menu' toolbar item, CKEditor5 plugin ai_balloon_menu.AiBalloonMenu) that appears when text is selected; enabled by adding its button to the text format's CKEditor 5 toolbar.
